list.text: ((48x+144)/8) ​ − (c/15)=d(x−2)             In the equation above,  c and  d are ⁣‍‌‌‍‌‌‍‌‌‌‍‌‍‍‌‍‌‍‍‍‍‌‍‍‍‍‍‌‍‍‍‍‌‌‍‌‍‌‌‌‌‌‌‍⁤constants and c>0. If the equation has infinitely many solutions, what is the value of c?

for the equation to have infinitely many solutions, the value of "d" must be 6, as 48x/6 is equal to 6(x). therefore, ((48x+144)/8)-(c/15)=6(x-2). The equation can be rearranged and simplified to get c/15=(144/8)+12. This can be solved to get c=450

This can be solved using a regression after it is determined that d=6. The equation "((48(x_1)+144)/8)-c/15~6((x_1)-2)" can be used to find c.  Using the equation "((48(x_1)+144)/8)-c/15~d((x_1)-2)" is insufficient due to the amount of unknown values in the equation causing the regression to yield a wrong answer for the value of c.
